files.text: epic! I Can Find It Spring Surprise WRITTEN BY Kim Anderson ILLUSTRATED BY Jessica Gibson
Jake hears the doorbell ring. He skips to the front door and swings it wide open. It is Aunt Hazel!
Jake and Aunt Hazel are going on a walk. They will take pictures of all the springtime things they see. The pictures are for a special surprise. Come along and help!

Jake spots four blue robin eggs in a grassy nest. He wants to take a picture! One egg has five speckles, just like how Jake has five freckles. Jake is so excited for the eggs to hatch. Can you find the blue robin egg with five speckles?
Next to the trail, Jake sees fiddlehead ferns, his favorite springtime plant! The young fern fronds look like twirly-whirly tails. Do you see twirly-whirly fiddlehead ferns?

Aunt Hazel helps Jake roll over a log. There are lots of creepy, crawly critters underneath! Jake takes a picture of a plump, green caterpillar with tiny, red spots. Do you see a plump, green caterpillar with tiny, red spots?

As they turn the corner, they see a deer family in the woods! The fawn has rusty-brown fur with bright white spots. Jake and Aunt Hazel make sure not to get too close as they snap a picture. Can you find the fawn with brown fur and white spots?

Jake looks for the pointy, purple-red leaves of a very special maple tree. Aunt Hazel planted this tree when Jake was born. It is only six years old, so it is shorter than most of the other trees. Do you see a short tree with pointy, purple-red leaves?
Jake finds the perfect mud puddle for splashing! It is big and round, but not too deep, and has some grass growing out of it. Can you find a big, round mud puddle?

At the edge of the lake, Jake spots a spring peeper frog and her eggs! the frog is orange and has a mark like an X on her back. Her eggs are clear with black dots in the middle. Do you see an orange frog with an X on her back and clear eggs with black dots?

As Jake and Aunt Hazel walk back to The house, they see a big, fluffy cloud in The shape of a lamb! The cloud sails across The blue sky above them. Jake takes one last picture. Do you see a big, fluffy cloud shaped like a lamb?
Aunt Hazel helps Jake put all the pictures they took into a photo album to share with his new baby sister, Hannah! He tells her about each of the plants and animals he saw.
Jake cannot wait to take walks with Aunt Hazel and baby Hannah when she gets bigger.
